Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We are going to construct an accepting Petri net for the insurance claim process described

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets break down the process Steps 1 Claim Registration CR 2 Document Verification DV 3 Fraud Check FC 4 Claim Assessment CA 5 Approval AP 6 Payment P 7 Notification N 8 Closure CL

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Approval AP only if amount surpasses threshold but note the description says must be approved by a senior manager if the amount surpasses a certain threshold However the process does not specify what happens if it does not surpass

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We assume that the approval step is conditional and might be skipped in some cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But the description does not explicitly say that the process can skip the Approval step Since the process description is fixed we assume that the Approval step is always performed

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Additionally there are two possible loops Between DV and RDS if documents are incomplete then we have a re submission and then back to DV Between FC and II if Fraud Check raises doubts then we do an In depth Investigation II and then go back to FC This can happen multiple times

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are going to create places and transitions accordingly

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets list the activities transitions we need CR DV FC CA AP P N CL RDS II

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Well create places between these transitions to model the state

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We note that the initial marking should have a token in a start place say p start and the final marking should have a token in a place that represents the closed claim say p end

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets design the Petri net We can think of the process as a sequence with two loops

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Well create the following places states p start p after CR p after DV p after FC p after II p after CA p after AP p after P p after N p end

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For the DV RDS loop we have a place after DV that can lead to either FC or RDS Then after RDS we go back to DV

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Similarly for the FC II loop p after FC to CA if no doubts p after FC to II if doubts Then after II we go back to FC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But wait in Petri nets transitions are the actions and places are states We do not label places we label transitions

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are going to have transitions labeled t CR label CR t DV label DV t FC label FC t CA label CA t AP label AP t P label P t N label N t CL label CL t RDS label RDS t II label II

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Places p start p after CR p after DV p after FC p after CA p after AP p after P p after N p end

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For the loops we need additional places For the DV RDS loop t RDS leads to a new place say p after RDS which then leads to t DV again For the FC II loop t II leads to a new place say p after II which then leads to t FC again

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The condition for taking the loop is nondeterministic In Petri nets we can model choice by having multiple transitions from a place

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are going to create the following arcs From p start to t CR Then p start to t CR to p after CR p after CR to t DV to p after DV From p after DV we have two choices p after DV to t FC to p after FC and p after DV to t RDS to p after RDS Then p after RDS to t DV to p after DV From p after FC we have two choices p after FC to t CA to p after CA and p after FC to t II to p after II Then p after II to t FC to p after FC Then continue p after CA to t AP to p after AP p after AP to t P to p after P p after P to t N to p after N p after N to t CL to p end

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The final marking is a token in p end The loops are not forced they are choices The Petri net allows both paths The in depth investigation can be repeated any number of times

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now lets code this in Python using pm4py Steps in code 1 Create a PetriNet object 2 Create places and transitions 3 Add them to the net 4 Create arcs according to the above structure 5 Create the initial marking with a token in p start and the final marking with a token in p end
